page: Microsoft Azure M64bs_v3 is a memory-optimized virtual machine belonging to the MBSMediumMemoryv3 family. It is powered by an Intel Xeon 8473C processor utilizing the x86_64 architecture. The server features 64 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 32 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled. It operates at a clock speed of 4.0 GHz and includes 105 MB of L3 cache. The system is equipped with 512.0 GB of RAM, providing an 8.0 GB of memory per core ratio. There is no local storage bundled with this instance, but it is Premium Storage capable and designed for block storage performance. No GPUs are present.

In benchmark testing, the M64bs_v3 demonstrates strong memory and processing capabilities. Its memory bandwidth is highly rated, achieving 4509.44 GB/sec for cached small-block reads and 216.83 GB/sec for uncached large-block reads. Single-core performance is notable, with Geekbench single-core scores reaching the top 10% tier at 2174.0, alongside strong multi-core performance. Cryptographic and compression tests also show strong results, with a Passmark CPU encryption score of 54921.56 and single-threaded gzip compression at 33.18 GB/sec. For database operations, the instance processed 71.51 million Redis SET operations per second.

From a resource density perspective, the M64bs_v3 offers a high-capacity memory profile relative to its compute cores, making it qualitatively cost-efficient for memory-bound workloads that do not require local NVMe storage. However, the lack of local storage means all storage operations must rely on external block storage, which may introduce latency tradeoffs depending on the attached volume type. The instance is highly suited for memory-intensive workloads, including relational databases, in-memory caching systems like Redis, software compilation, and medium-scale LLM inference tasks.
description: The Microsoft Azure M64bs_v3 is a memory-optimized virtual machine from the MBSMediumMemoryv3 family, powered by a 4.0 GHz Intel Xeon 8473C processor. Utilizing the x86_64 architecture, it provides 64 dedicated vCPUs and 512.0 GB of RAM, establishing an 8.0 GB per core memory ratio. The server does not include local storage but is Premium Storage capable for external block storage needs. Benchmark results highlight strong memory bandwidth, with uncached large-block reads at 216.83 GB/sec, and top-tier single-core performance. It also demonstrates strong database capabilities, executing 71.51 million Redis SET operations per second. Lacking a GPU and local storage, this instance is qualitatively cost-efficient for memory-heavy, non-local storage-dependent workloads. It is highly suited for enterprise databases, large-scale in-memory caching, and software compilation.
og_description: Microsoft Azure M64bs_v3 is a memory-optimized server featuring 64 vCPUs, 512 GB RAM, and an Intel Xeon 8473C processor. It delivers strong memory bandwidth and database performance without local storage.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure M64bs_v3 is a memory-optimized server with 64 vCPUs, 512 GB RAM, and an Intel Xeon processor, built for high-performance database workloads.
tagline: A memory-optimized virtual machine delivering high-bandwidth RAM and dedicated Intel Xeon processing for demanding database and caching workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by a 4.0 GHz Intel Xeon 8473C processor with 64 dedicated vCPUs
Equipped with 512.0 GB of RAM, offering an 8.0 GB per core ratio
No local storage included; relies on external Premium Block Storage
Strong memory bandwidth reaching 216.83 GB/sec for uncached large-block reads
Best suited for enterprise databases, Redis caching, and software compilation
